metadata.summary: Interpolation functions for heightmaps developed within the EMODnet Bathymetry (High Resolution Seabed Mapping) project.

documentation:
# EMODnet - Heightmap Interpolation (Python)

Interpolation functions for heightmaps developed within the EMODnet Bathymetry (High Resolution Seabed Mapping) project.

Please visit the documentation at: https://emodnet-heightmap-interpolation.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## Installation

This package is available through [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/heightmap-interpolation/):

```
pip install heightmap-interpolation
```

Otherwise, the package and all its requirements can be installed from sources through `setuptools` using:

```
python setup.py install 
```

Note that it requires python3.7 to run.

If you prefer to install it in a virtual environment:

```
python3.7 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
pip install --upgrade pip
pip install .
```

## Usage

After installation, you should have the main entry point for interpolating NetCDF4 datasets already in the path, so you can call:

```
interpolate_netcdf4 --help
```

Since this package was developed within the EMODnet Bathymetry project, for the moment no other inputs are expected. For other inputs, take the code in the `apps/interpolate_netcdf4.py` script as reference and use directly the different interpolation modules at your convenience. 

## Docker

For convenience, we also provide a docker image with all the dependencies intalled at DockerHub. Assuming you have docker installed, you can obtain it by:

```
docker pull coroniscomputing/heightmap_interpolation:<tag_name>
```

Where `<tag_name>` must be a specific version of the package, or `latest`.

Or, if you want to compile the docker image by yourself:

```
docker build -t <image_tag_name> .
```

Then, run it with:

```
docker run -it -v <data_folder>:/data coroniscomputing/heightmap_interpolation:<tag_name>
```

On the one hand, using the `-v` flag we are mounting the directory containing the data to process to the `/data` folder within the container. The container will automatically run the `bash` command, and you will be inside the container. Thus, there we simply run the `interpolate_netcdf4` script with the desired parameters

For instance:

```
interpolate_netcdf4 -o /data/<netcdf_results_file> linear /data/<netcdf_input_file>
```  

Keep in mind that this way of running the docker does not provide visualization, so the "--show" flag will be useless! There are ways of sharing the Xs with docker, but these are out of the scope of this documentation.

## Acknowledgements

This project has been developed by Coronis Computing S.L. within the EMODnet Bathymetry (High Resolution Seabed Mapping) project.

* EMODnet: http://www.emodnet.eu/
* EMODnet (bathymetry): http://www.emodnet-bathymetry.eu/
* Coronis: http://www.coronis.es

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.interpolants.quad_tree_pu_rbf_interpolant.QTNode.contains
documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.inpaint_multigrid
documentation:
Multigrid solver for inpainting problems.

Args:
    img: input image to be inpainted
    mask: logical mask of the same size as the input image.
          True == known pixels, False == unknown pixels to be inpainted
Returns:
    inpainted image

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

documentation:
Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Implements the method in:
  Smith, W. H. F, and P. Wessel, 1990, Gridding with continuous curvature splines in tension, Geophysics, 55, 293-305.

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.tension_spline_rbf.tension_spline_rbf
documentation:
Spline with tension.

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 0 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

    x, y: Coordinates of the points to interpolate

Returns:
    Interpolated values at (x, y)
This way of defining Abstract classes requires Python 3.4+

Abstract base class for Interpolant(s)

Attributes:
    data: The known x, y, z values of the function to interpolate (size numPts x 3)

documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

Multigrid solver for inpainting problems.

Args:

Returns:

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Implements the method in:
  Smith, W. H. F, and P. Wessel, 1990, Gridding with continuous curvature splines in tension, Geophysics, 55, 293-305.

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

Remove points stored at non-leaf nodes

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.interpolants.quad_tree_pu_rbf_interpolant.QTNode.contains
documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: build.lib.old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

classes.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.DifferentialOperators

methods.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.DifferentialOperators.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.
Differential operators implemented using a custom convolver

classes.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.ccst_inpainter.CCSTInpainter
documentation:
Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Implements the method in:
  Smith, W. H. F, and P. Wessel, 1990, Gridding with continuous curvature splines in tension, Geophysics, 55, 293-305.

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

classes.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter

methods.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    term_check_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    term_thres (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    term_check_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    term_thres (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.convolve_at_mask

@njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]), parallel=True, nogil=True, fastmath=True)

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask
documentation: Fast 2D convolution using Numba, but applied just at the points defined by the mask with the same behaviour at borders as OpenCV's filter2D default behaviour (BORDER_REFLECT_101).

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_guvec
documentation: Fast 2D convolution using Numba as a guvector, but applied just at the points defined by the mask with the same behaviour at borders as OpenCV's filter2D default behaviour (BORDER_REFLECT_101).

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_parallel
documentation: Same as nb_convolve_at_mask, but with parallelized fors (the parallelization overhead may render this method slower for small images)

functions.name: build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.interpolants.quad_tree_pu_rbf_interpolant.QTNode.contains
documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.interpolants.quad_tree_pu_rbf_interpolant.QTNode.contains
documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.reporter.deprecated.inpaint_netcdf_reporter.InpaintingReporter.write_intro_section
documentation: def prepare_section(self, section_name):
section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)
doc_path = os.path.join(section_dir, section_name+".md")
if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:
return None, None
os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Implements the method in:
  Smith, W. H. F, and P. Wessel, 1990, Gridding with continuous curvature splines in tension, Geophysics, 55, 293-305.

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

section_dir = os.path.join(self.docs_dir, section_name)

if os.path.exists(doc_path) and not self.config["re-write"]:

os.makedirs(section_dir, exist_ok=True)
return section_dir, doc_path

classes.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.inpaint_multigrid
documentation:
Multigrid solver for inpainting problems.

Args:
    img: input image to be inpainted
    mask: logical mask of the same size as the input image.
          True == known pixels, False == unknown pixels to be inpainted
Returns:
    inpainted image

methods.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

classes.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.ccst_inpainter.CCSTInpainter
documentation:
Continous Curvature Splines in Tension (CCST) inpainter

Implements the method in:
  Smith, W. H. F, and P. Wessel, 1990, Gridding with continuous curvature splines in tension, Geophysics, 55, 293-305.

Should mimic GMT surface (http://gmt.soest.hawaii.edu/doc/latest/surface.html)

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real

functions.name: old.build.lib.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.convolve_at_mask.nb_convolve_at_mask_cuda
documentation: # The numba-speeded up version
# nb_filter2d_at_mask = njit(double[:, :](double[:, :], boolean[:, :], double[:, :]))(filter2d_at_mask, parallel=True)
# Now fastfilter_2d runs at speeds as if you had first translated
# it to C, compiled the code and wrapped it with Python
width = 10000
height = 10000
shape = (height, width)
image = numpy.random.random(shape)
filt = numpy.random.random((3, 3))
mask = numpy.ones(shape, dtype=bool)
mask[:, 0:(width//4)*3] = False
# ts = timer()
# res = filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
# te = timer()
# print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
# Force numba compilation before timing
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(numpy.random.random((3, 3)), numpy.ones((3, 3), dtype=bool), numpy.random.random((3, 3)))
ts = timer()
res = nb_filter2d_at_mask(image, mask, filt)
te = timer()
print("nb_filter2d_at_mask = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
ts = timer()
res = cv2.filter2D(image, -1, filt)
te = timer()
print("cv2.filter2D = {:.2f} sec.".format(te - ts))
--- CUDA version ---

documentation: Check if the input node domain is completely contained in the domain of this node

QuadTree Node of the QuadTreePURBF interpolant

Attributes:
    x: center of the node in X
    y: center of the node in Y
    wh: width/height of the node (nodes are squares!)
    h: height of the node
    pts: points in this node (of size numPts x 3)
    childs: children of the node (up to 4)
    rbf_interp: the interpolator function (just computed at leaves)
    weighting_rbf: the weighting CSRBF
    dist_fun: distance function between points in the XY plane
    overlap: amount of overlap between circles (scalar between 0 and 1). The base radius of a cell (overlap = 0) in the QuadTree corresponds to half the length of its diagonal. This parameter is a multiplicative factor applied to this base radius.

functions.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.regularized_spline_rbf.regularized_spline_rbf
documentation:
Regularized spline

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 1 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

functions.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.apps.netcdf_data_io.load_interpolation_input_data
documentation:
Loads the data required for interpolation from the NetCDF file

```
param input_file

path to the input file (expected NetCDF4 format)

param elevation_var

the name of the variable within the netcdf4 file representing the elevation

param interpolation_flag_var

the name of the variable representing an "interpolation flag", where 0 == reference data, 1 == interpolated point (or point to interpolate).

param areas_kml_file

the KML file with a set of areas where the interpolation should take place. Areas out of the described polygons will be left as they are.
```

functions.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.rbf.tension_spline_rbf.tension_spline_rbf
documentation:
Spline with tension.

Definition of the spline with tension from [2]. You can also find a brief description of its behaviour in [1].
Note that [1] suggests using a polynomial of degree 0 with this RBF.

Args:
    r: value to evaluate.
    e: tension parameter.

Returns:
    value of the RBF at r.

References:
[1] https://pro.arcgis.com/en/pro-app/tool-reference/spatial-analyst/how-spline-works.htm
[2] Mitas, L., and H. Mitasova. 1988. General Variational Approach to the Interpolation Problem. Comput. Math. Applic. Vol. 16. No. 12. pp. 983–992. Great Britain.

methods.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.__init__
documentation:
Constructor

Keyword Args:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

Multigrid solver for inpainting problems.

Returns:

methods.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.fd_pde_inpainter.FDPDEInpainter.print_start
documentation: Printing utilities...

Abstract base class for Finite-Differences Partial Differential Equation (FDPDE) Inpainters

Common interphase for PDE-based inpainting methods. Solves the problem using finite differences in a gradient-descent manner.

Attributes:
    dt (float): Gradient descent step size.
    rel_change_iters (int): Check the relative change between iterations of the optimizer every this number of iterations.
    rel_change_tolerance (float): Stop the optimization when the energy descent between iterations is less than
    max_iters (int): Maximum number of iterations for the optimizer.
    relaxation (float): Over-relaxation parameter. It is still under testing, use with care.
    mgs_levels (int): Number of levels of detail to use in the Mult-Grid Solver (MGS). Setting it to 1 deactivates the MGS.
    mgs_min_res (int): minimum resolution (width or height) allowed for a level in the MGS. If the level of detail in the pyramid gets to a value lower than this, the pyramid construction will stop.
    print_progress (bool): Print information about the progress of the optimization on screen.
    print_progress_iters (int): If print_progress==True, the information will be printed every this number of iterations.
    init_with (str): initializer for the unknown data before applying the optimization.
    convolver_type (str): the convolver used for all the convolutions required by the solver.
    debug_dir (str): a debug directory where the intermediate steps will be rendered. Useful to create a video of the evolution of the solver.

functions.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.divergence
documentation:
Computes the divergence of the vector field f, corresponding to dFx/dx + dFy/dy + ...

Code extracted from:
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/11435809/compute-divergence-of-vector-field-using-python (Daniel's answer)
    https://stackoverflow.com/questions/67970477/compute-divergence-with-python/67971515#67971515

Args:
    f: List of ndarrays, where every item of the list is one dimension of the vector field.

Returns:
    Single ndarray of the same shape as each of the items in f, which corresponds to a scalar field.

functions.name: old.build.lib.heightmap_interpolation.inpainting.differential.my_laplacian
documentation: Code from https://stackoverflow.com/questions/40703751/using-fourier-transforms-to-do-convolution
def np_fftconvolve(x, y):
s1 = np.array(x.shape)
s2 = np.array(y.shape)
size = s1 + s2 - 1
fsize = 2 ** np.ceil(np.log2(size)).astype(int)
fslice = tuple([slice(0, int(sz)) for sz in size])
new_x = np.fft.fft2(x, fsize)
new_y = np.fft.fft2(y, fsize)
result = np.fft.ifft2(new_x * new_y)[fslice].copy()
return result.real
